rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARTER ET AL VS. SOCO WEST, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 19, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Clark Equipment Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. The Court must liberally construe plaintiffs' evidence and make all reasonable inferences in plaintiffs' favor. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Carter was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Carter, Earl Signater and Solomon Orona preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Tom Ihringer are sustained. However even if Mr. Ihringer's declaration is admissible, it is not sufficient to sustain defendant's initial burden. However, if defendant did sustain its initial burden, all the evidence before the Court, specifically the deposition testimony of Mr. Carter, Earl Signater and Solomon Orona, create a triable issue whether Mr. Carter was exposed to asbestos-containing brakes attributable to defendant. The Court did not consider defendant's reply papers for failure to lodge a courtesy copy with the Court. (Local Rules of Court, Rule 2.11(T).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
